findings: A strong dependency cluster exists among features related to packet size and segment size metrics, with correlations at or near 1, indicating high redundancy.
Dependency contextualization of representative features confirms concentrated dependency profiles within this cluster.
These structural redundancies imply that deduplication would reduce dependency concentration and alter feature importance distributions within this correlated set.
limitations: Analysis limited to local dependency cluster; global effects on feature importance shifts post-deduplication remain unobserved.
